mnemonics.title: Coset Partitioning
mnemonics.content: CHIP: Cosets are Disjoint, they Help partition the group. Each element belongs to exactly ONE coset.

mnemonics.title: Normal Subgroup Test
mnemonics.content: NORMAL: Na = aN for ALL a. If conjugates stay in N (gng⁻¹ ∈ N), then N is normal.

mnemonics.title: Lagrange's Formula
mnemonics.content: GIrving's Formula: |G| = Index × |H| → G = I × H. Think: Group equals Index times H.

mnemonics.title: Coset Equality
mnemonics.content: A-OK: aH = bH iff a⁻¹b ∈ H. If a-inverse-b is in H, we're OK!

acronyms.usage: N ◁ G iff aN = Na for all a ∈ G

acronyms.expansion: Coset Similarity Index: All cosets same size as H
acronyms.usage: Checking coset sizes in finite groups

visualTips.visualization: Imagine H as a 'grid line' and cosets as parallel lines obtained by shifting H by each group element. Each coset is a 'parallel copy' of H.
Think of group G as a pizza cut into equal slices by subgroup H. Each slice is one coset - all same size, no overlap.
Normal subgroup N 'collapses' into a single point in the quotient. G/N is like a 'photo' of G where all elements in same coset look identical.
Index [G:H] tells how many times H 'expands' to fill G. Like tiles covering a floor - H is the tile, index tells how many tiles.

analogies.analogy: Like hotel rooms: H is a room layout, each coset is that same layout in a different room number. All rooms look the same, just different 'address'.
A normal subgroup is like a 'transparent wall' - going through it from left or right gives the same result. Cosets behave identically from both sides.
Think of modulo arithmetic like a clock: Z₁₂ 'collapses' hours 1-12 into positions. Each hour is a coset of the subgroup {0, 6}.
Index is like 'magnification': if H is 1/4 the size of G, we need 4 'copies' (cosets) to reconstruct G. Index = magnification factor.
